description: Visualize DDB1-binding interface conservation analysis showing the H-box helix divergence
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import numpy as np

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 7))
fig.suptitle('DCAF12L2 DDB1-Binding Interface Conservation Analysis', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')

# Panel A: Interface residue conservation by region
ax = axes[0]

# Data from the analysis
regions = ['N-terminal\nhelix (H-box)\n27 residues', 'WD1-2\n3 residues', 'WD3-6\n1 residue', 'WD7/C-term\n5 residues']
identical_counts = [15, 2, 1, 2]
conservative_counts = [1, 0, 0, 0]
noncons_counts = [11, 1, 0, 3]

x = np.arange(len(regions))
width = 0.25

bars1 = ax.bar(x - width, identical_counts, width, label='Identical', color='#2ecc71')
bars2 = ax.bar(x, conservative_counts, width, label='Conservative', color='#f39c12')
bars3 = ax.bar(x + width, noncons_counts, width, label='Non-conservative', color='#e74c3c')

ax.set_xlabel('Interface Region')
ax.set_ylabel('Number of residues')
ax.set_title('A. DDB1-Contacting Residue Conservation by Region')
ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(regions, fontsize=8)
ax.legend()

# Add percentage annotation
for i, (ident, cons, nc) in enumerate(zip(identical_counts, conservative_counts, noncons_counts)):
    total = ident + cons + nc
    pct = (ident + cons) / total * 100 if total > 0 else 0
    ax.text(i, max(ident, cons, nc) + 0.5, f'{pct:.0f}% conserved', ha='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')

# Panel B: Schematic of interface mapping
ax = axes[1]
ax.axis('off')

# Create a schematic showing DCAF12 structure with highlighted interface
text = """
DDB1-DCAF12 Interface Analysis (from PDB 8AJN, cryo-EM 3.0Å)

KEY FINDINGS:

1. DDB1-binding interface has 36 contact residues
   • 56% identical (20/36)
   • 3% conservative (1/36)  
   • 42% non-conservative (15/36)
   Overall: 58% conserved (< 66.6% global identity)

2. N-terminal H-box helix (res 42-80) is the MAJOR interface
   • 27 of 36 contact residues are in this region
   • Several disruptive substitutions:
     Y48→H  (aromatic → imidazole)
     L57→P  (helix → helix-breaker!)  
     E60→L  (charged → hydrophobic)
     H68→R  (imidazole → guanidinium)
     
3. YDIR motif (Y365-R368) is NOT at the direct interface
   • Mutagenesis R368 affects DDB1 binding (allosteric?)
   • YDIR is perfectly conserved in DCAF12L2

4. WD40 propeller periphery: mixed conservation
   • H100→A (loss of histidine at WD1 contact)
   • S428→W, S429→P, T431→M (WD7 changes)

IMPLICATION: DCAF12L2 DDB1 binding is UNCERTAIN
despite YDIR conservation, because the actual
contact surface (H-box) shows significant divergence.
"""
ax.text(0.05, 0.95, text, transform=ax.transAxes, fontsize=9,
        verticalalignment='top', fontfamily='monospace',
        bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='lightyellow', alpha=0.8))
ax.set_title('B. Interface Analysis Summary', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('dcaf12l2_interface_analysis.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("Figure saved")
